001package guiNewAccount;
002
003import javafx.geometry.Pos;
004import javafx.scene.Scene;
005import javafx.scene.control.Alert;
006import javafx.scene.control.Button;
007import javafx.scene.control.Label;
008import javafx.scene.control.PasswordField;
009import javafx.scene.control.TextField;
010import javafx.scene.control.Alert.AlertType;
011import javafx.scene.layout.Pane;
012import javafx.scene.text.Font;
013import javafx.stage.Stage;
014import database.Database;
015import entityClasses.User;
016
017/*******
018 * <p> Title: ViewNewAccount Class. </p>
019 * 
020 * <p> Description: The ViewNewAccount Page is used to enable a potential user with an invitation
021 * code to establish an account after they have specified an invitation code on the standard login
022 * page. </p>
023 * 
024 * <p> Copyright: Lynn Robert Carter © 2025 </p>
025 * 
026 * @author Lynn Robert Carter
027 * 
028 * @version 1.00                2025-08-19 Initial version
029 *  
030 */
031
032public class ViewNewAccount {
033        
034        /*-********************************************************************************************
035
036        Attributes
037        
038        */
039        
040        // These are the application values required by the user interface
041        
042        private static double width = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.WINDOW_WIDTH;
043        private static double height = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.WINDOW_HEIGHT;
044        
045        // This is a simple GUI login Page, very similar to the FirstAdmin login page.  The only real
046        // difference is in this case we also know an email address, since it was used to send the
047        // invitation to the potential user.
048        private static Label label_ApplicationTitle = 
049                        new Label("Foundation Application Account Setup Page");
050    protected static Label label_NewUserCreation = new Label(" User Account Creation.");
051    protected static Label label_NewUserLine = new Label("Please enter a username and a password.");
052    protected static TextField text_Username = new TextField();
053    protected static PasswordField text_Password1 = new PasswordField();
054    protected static PasswordField text_Password2 = new PasswordField();
055    protected static Button button_UserSetup = new Button("User Setup");
056    protected static TextField text_Invitation = new TextField();
057
058        // This alert is used should the invitation code be invalid
059    protected static Alert alertInvitationCodeIsInvalid = new Alert(AlertType.INFORMATION);
060
061        // This alert is used should the user enter two passwords that do not match
062        protected static Alert alertUsernamePasswordError = new Alert(AlertType.INFORMATION);
063
064    protected static Button button_Quit = new Button("Quit");
065
066        // These attributes are used to configure the page and populate it with this user's information
067        private static ViewNewAccount theView;          // Is instantiation of the class needed?
068
069        // Reference for the in-memory database so this package has access
070        private static Database theDatabase = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.database;         
071
072        protected static Stage theStage;                        // The Stage that JavaFX has established for us
073        private static Pane theRootPane;                        // The Pane that holds all the GUI widgets 
074        protected static User theUser;                          // The current logged in User
075   
076    protected static String theInvitationCode;  // The invitation code links to an email address
077                                                                                        // and a role for this user
078    protected static String emailAddress;               // Established here for use by the controller
079    protected static String theRole;                    // Established here for use by the controller
080        public static Scene theNewAccountScene = null;  // Access to the User Update page's GUI Widgets
081        
082
083        /*-********************************************************************************************
084
085        Constructors
086        
087        */
088
089        /**********
090         * <p> Method: displayNewAccount(Stage ps, String ic) </p>
091         * 
092         * <p> Description: This method is the single entry point from outside this package to cause
093         * the NewAccount page to be displayed.
094         * 
095         * It first sets up very shared attributes so we don't have to pass parameters.
096         * 
097         * It then checks to see if the page has been setup.  If not, it instantiates the class, 
098         * initializes all the static aspects of the GUI widgets (e.g., location on the page, font,
099         * size, and any methods to be performed).
100         * 
101         * After the instantiation, the code then populates the elements that change based on the user
102         * and the system's current state.  It then sets the Scene onto the stage, and makes it visible
103         * to the user.
104         * 
105         * @param ps specifies the JavaFX Stage to be used for this GUI and it's methods
106         * 
107         * @param ic specifies the user's invitation code for this GUI and it's methods
108         * 
109         */
110        public static void displayNewAccount(Stage ps, String ic) {
111                // This is the only way some component of the system can cause a New User Account page to
112                // appear.  The first time, the class is created and initialized.  Every subsequent call it
113                // is reused with only the elements that differ being initialized.
114                
115                // Establish the references to the GUI and the current user
116                theStage = ps;                          // Save the reference to the Stage for the rest of this package
117                theInvitationCode = ic;         // Establish the invitation code so it can be easily accessed
118                
119                if (theView == null) theView = new ViewNewAccount();
120                
121                text_Username.setText("");      // Clear the input fields so previously entered values do not
122                text_Password1.setText("");     // appear for a new user
123                text_Password2.setText("");
124                
125                // Fetch the role for this user
126                theRole = theDatabase.getRoleGivenAnInvitationCode(theInvitationCode);
127                
128                if (theRole.length() == 0) {// If there is an issue with the invitation code, display a
129                        alertInvitationCodeIsInvalid.showAndWait();     // dialog box saying that are when it it
130                        return;                                 // acknowledged, return so the proper code can be entered
131                }
132                
133                // Get the email address associated with the invitation code
134                emailAddress = theDatabase.getEmailAddressUsingCode(theInvitationCode);
135                
136        // Place all of the established GUI elements into the pane
137        theRootPane.getChildren().clear();
138        theRootPane.getChildren().addAll(label_NewUserCreation, label_NewUserLine, text_Username,
139                        text_Password1, text_Password2, button_UserSetup, button_Quit);         
140
141                // Set the title for the window, display the page, and wait for the Admin to do something
142                theStage.setTitle("CSE 360 Foundation Code: New User Account Setup");   
143        theStage.setScene(theNewAccountScene);
144                theStage.show();
145        }
146        
147        /**********
148         * <p> Constructor: ViewNewAccount() </p>
149         * 
150         * <p> Description: This constructor is called just once, the first time a new account needs to
151         * be created.  It establishes all of the common GUI widgets for the page so they are only
152         * created once and reused when needed.
153         * 
154         * The do
155         *              
156         */
157        private ViewNewAccount() {
158                
159                // Create the Pane for the list of widgets and the Scene for the window
160                theRootPane = new Pane();
161                theNewAccountScene = new Scene(theRootPane, width, height);
162
163                // Label the Panle with the name of the startup screen, centered at the top of the pane
164                setupLabelUI(label_ApplicationTitle, "Arial", 28, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 5);
165                
166        // Label to display the welcome message for the new user
167        setupLabelUI(label_NewUserCreation, "Arial", 32, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 10);
168        
169        // Label to display the  message for the first user
170        setupLabelUI(label_NewUserLine, "Arial", 24, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 70);
171                
172                // Establish the text input operand asking for a username
173                setupTextUI(text_Username, "Arial", 18, 300, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 50, 160, true);
174                text_Username.setPromptText("Enter the Username");
175                
176                // Establish the text input operand field for the password
177                setupTextUI(text_Password1, "Arial", 18, 300, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 50, 210, true);
178                text_Password1.setPromptText("Enter the Password");
179                
180                // Establish the text input operand field to confirm the password
181                setupTextUI(text_Password2, "Arial", 18, 300, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 50, 260, true);
182                text_Password2.setPromptText("Enter the Password Again");
183                
184                // If the invitation code is wrong, this alert dialog will tell the user
185                alertInvitationCodeIsInvalid.setTitle("Invalid Invitation Code");
186                alertInvitationCodeIsInvalid.setHeaderText("The invitation code is not valid.");
187                alertInvitationCodeIsInvalid.setContentText("Correct the code and try again.");
188
189                // If the passwords do not match, this alert dialog will tell the user
190                alertUsernamePasswordError.setTitle("Passwords Do Not Match");
191                alertUsernamePasswordError.setHeaderText("The two passwords must be identical.");
192                alertUsernamePasswordError.setContentText("Correct the passwords and try again.");
193
194        // Set up the account creation and login
195        setupButtonUI(button_UserSetup, "Dialog", 18, 200, Pos.CENTER, 475, 210);
196        button_UserSetup.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerNewAccount.doCreateUser(); });
197                
198        // Enable the user to quit the application
199        setupButtonUI(button_Quit, "Dialog", 18, 250, Pos.CENTER, 300, 540);
200        button_Quit.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerNewAccount.performQuit(); });
201        }
202        
203        
204        /*-********************************************************************************************
205
206        Helper methods to reduce code length
207
208         */
209        
210        /**********
211         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a label
212         */
213        
214        private void setupLabelUI(Label l, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y){
215                l.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
216                l.setMinWidth(w);
217                l.setAlignment(p);
218                l.setLayoutX(x);
219                l.setLayoutY(y);                
220        }
221        
222        
223        /**********
224         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a button
225         * 
226         * @param b             The Button object to be initialized
227         * @param ff    The font to be used
228         * @param f             The size of the font to be used
229         * @param w             The width of the Button
230         * @param p             The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
231         * @param x             The location from the left edge (x axis)
232         * @param y             The location from the top (y axis)
233         */
234        private void setupButtonUI(Button b, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y){
235                b.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
236                b.setMinWidth(w);
237                b.setAlignment(p);
238                b.setLayoutX(x);
239                b.setLayoutY(y);                
240        }
241
242        /**********
243         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a text field
244         */
245        private void setupTextUI(TextField t, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y, boolean e){
246                t.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
247                t.setMinWidth(w);
248                t.setMaxWidth(w);
249                t.setAlignment(p);
250                t.setLayoutX(x);
251                t.setLayoutY(y);                
252                t.setEditable(e);
253        }       
254}
